# Nanook

> Nanook is an open-source toolkit for test case and test data creation. It combines equivalence class tables with data generators to systematically define, document, and generate test data for software testing.

## Documentation
- [Quickstart Guide](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/quickstart/quickstart): 5-minute introduction to creating equivalence class tables and generating test data
- [General Overview](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/guide/generalOverview): Architecture and concepts of Nanook Table
- [Equivalence Class Tables](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/guide/equivalence/overview): How to define test cases using equivalence classes
- [Matrix Tables](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/guide/matrix/overview): Cartesian product test case generation
- [Data Generators](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/guide/generatrorCommand/generator): Custom data generator functions
- [Static Data](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/guide/generatrorCommand/static): Static test data definitions
- [References](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/guide/generatrorCommand/reference): Cross-table data references

## Tutorials
- [Create Equivalence Class Table](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/tutorials/createEquivalenceClassTable): Step-by-step guide
- [Transform to Data Generator](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/tutorials/transform2dataGenerator): Convert tables to generators
- [Create Data Generator](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/tutorials/createGenerator): Build custom generators
- [Create Writer](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/tutorials/createWriter): Export data in custom formats

## API Reference
- [Processor](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/api/processor/Processor): Core processing engine
- [Data Generator Base](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/api/dataGenerator/DataGeneratorBase): Base class for generators
- [Table Decision](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/api/model-decision/TableDecision): Decision table model
- [File Processor](https://nanook.xhub.io/docs/api/file-processor/FileProcessor): Spreadsheet file handling

## Key Facts
- Open source (GitHub: https://github.com/xhubio/nanook-table)
- Written in JavaScript/Node.js
- Reads test definitions from LibreOffice, Excel, or Google Sheets spreadsheets
- Generates test data in JSON, CSV, or any custom format
- Supports equivalence class partitioning and boundary value analysis
- Customizable generators and exporters via plugins
- Used by Deutsche Bahn and other enterprises
- Created by xhub.io

## Contact
- Website: https://nanook.xhub.io
- GitHub: https://github.com/xhubio/nanook-table
- Organization: xhub.io (https://xhub.io)
